div.p: William by the grace of God king of Scots to all worthy men of the whole of his land, clerk and lay, Greeting: Let present and future know that I have granted, and by this my charter made firm, the donation that Ada daughter of Alexander de St-Martin made to God and the church of Holyrood in Edinburgh, and to the canons serving God there now and in the future, of an income of half a merk yearly, to be received from the ferm of the mill at Athelstaneford. To be held as freely and quietly, fully and honourably, as the charter of the said Ada bears witness. Reserving my service. As witnesses, William del Bois my chancellor, Philip de Mowbray, Master Martin my physician, Gilbert of Stirling my clerk, Richard son of Hugh. At Edinburgh on the 22nd day of April.
